name: subregion_view_length
code:
proof fn phi_1_subregion_view_length<PMRegion: PersistentMemoryRegion>(
    pm: &PMRegion,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
)
    requires
        pm.inv(),
        start + len <= pm@.len() <= u64::MAX,
    ensures
        get_subregion_view(pm@, start as nat, len).len() == len,
{
}
reason: The subregion view length should equal the requested length — if subrange produced a different length, all offset-based reasoning within the subregion would be broken

name: subregion_start_cast_roundtrip
reason: The u64→nat→u64 roundtrip should be lossless — if the cast dropped high bits, the stored start_ field would not match the spec-level start()

name: subregion_view_byte_matches_region
code:
proof fn phi_3_subregion_view_byte_matches_region<PMRegion: PersistentMemoryRegion>(
    pm: &PMRegion,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
    k: int,
)
    requires
        pm.inv(),
        start + len <= pm@.len() <= u64::MAX,
        0 <= k < len,
    ensures
        get_subregion_view(pm@, start as nat, len).state[k] == pm@.state[start as int + k],
{
}
reason: Subregion byte at relative offset k should equal the region byte at absolute offset start+k — if the indexing were misaligned, reads through the subregion would access wrong physical bytes

name: full_region_subregion_equals_region
reason: A subregion spanning the entire region should equal the original — if it didn't, creating a full-coverage subregion would lose or alter data

name: adjacent_subregions_partition
code:
proof fn phi_5_adjacent_subregions_partition<PMRegion: PersistentMemoryRegion>(
    pm: &PMRegion,
    start: u64,
    len1: nat,
    len2: nat,
    k: int,
)
    requires
        pm.inv(),
        start + len1 + len2 <= pm@.len() <= u64::MAX,
        0 <= k < len1,
    ensures
        get_subregion_view(pm@, start as nat, len1 + len2).state[k]
            == get_subregion_view(pm@, start as nat, len1).state[k],
{
}
reason: The first portion of a combined subregion should match a subregion of just that portion — if they disagreed, splitting a subregion into two adjacent pieces would produce inconsistent views
